what_it_does: Catalogues named content slots (e.g., page-title, body-paragraph, stat-number) with character/word/line counts, representative examples, source deck or page, and compression-depth notes, forming a typographic and content-samples ledger across pitch decks and landing pages.
observations: Each entry is a content slot with metrics (chars, words, lines), an optional example, and provenance fields _src and _depth.

Sources include capital-raise (30-page expanded), company-info (18-page), deck1-2026 (15-page canonical), pitch-deck (15-page, 17 slides), presentation-15p, product-vi-landing (1-page + landing), and product-vt (multi-depth).
Many slots have explicit size or layout variants such as short, medium, long, 2line, centred, and split-layout.
Some entries carry notes about layout constraints, e.g., 'MUST fit 1 line' or 'max ~6 words in a 50% zone'.
Examples are verbatim text samples drawn from the source material.
The provided excerpt is truncated; product-vt entries at the end lack complete metric blocks.
No code symbols are present; the file is purely content/design-substrate metadata.
standouts: The same conceptual text role appears under different slot names across sources (e.g., slide-title, H1-slide-title, page-title), with different length variants.
Compression-depth metadata is explicit, contrasting 30-page expanded expression with 1-page collapsed expression and multi-depth product pages.
Constraint notes sometimes express stricter intent than the recorded metrics.
conventions: Slot names use hierarchical, semantic tokens separated by hyphens.
Every entry includes chars, words, and lines counts.
Provenance is consistently recorded via _src and _depth.
Optional 'note' fields capture layout or copy constraints not derivable from the metrics alone.
concerns: The file is truncated at the end, so product-vt entries are incomplete.
Similar slot names across sources are not normalized, which could create ambiguity if treated as canonical tokens without a mapping.
Metrics appear to be derived from a single observed example rather than stated min/max bounds.
notes: This is a content-samples inventory that maps every recurring text role in the design system to its observed length, a verbatim example, and the deck or page it came from.
questions: Are the recorded metrics maximums, medians, or single-instance counts?
Is the slot taxonomy intended to be canonical across all decks or only per-source?
How are line counts determined for multi-line entries (soft wrap vs explicit line break)?
What is the intended relationship between similar slots across different sources?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as a content-samples reference; it also implicitly serves as a typographic-scale and compression-depth map. No explicit stated purpose appears in the file, so actual use is inferred.
apparent_themes: Evidence-based typography measured in chars, words, and lines
intention_signals: It tries to make the design language portable and measurable by extracting every recurring text role into a reusable, source-annotated slot library, so writers and layout systems can choose the right length variant for each surface and depth.
novelty: The explicit pairing of content slots with compression-depth provenance (e.g., 30-page expanded vs 1-page collapsed) and the preservation of source-specific variant names alongside metrics.
contribution: A reusable inventory of content-length profiles and source-annotated examples that a fused system can consult when generating or validating copy for specific slots, depths, and surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of named content slots from pitch decks and landing pages, each annotated with character, word, and line counts, a representative example, source deck, and compression-depth context.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a measurable, source-annotated content-slot library for consistent copy generation and layout validation across multiple deck depths and page types.
